prompt:
Our doc-site hygiene auditor (siteaudit.ps1) flags oversize files, stale files, and sloppy names under the published root -- reproducible because the reference instant comes in as -Now. It has been running in the weekly job for months and other teams parse its output, so its current behavior is load-bearing. But the findings list has rotted into noise: the archive tree triggers on every run, everyone knows, nobody cares, and real problems scroll past.

Two additions from the review, existing output untouched:

1. A rules file, passed as `-Rules <file>`. One directive per line; blank lines and `#` comments ignored:
   - `exclude <pattern>` -- suppress every finding whose relative path matches the wildcard pattern.
   - `severity <level> <pattern>` -- set the level (info|warn|error) of any finding on a matching path.
   Patterns match the RELATIVE path and they match case-sensitively -- `Archive/*` does not touch `archive/`, we learned that lesson from another tool. Patterns may contain spaces (our file names do). Precedence, and I want this exact: an exclude always wins over any severity line; among severity lines that match the same path, the LAST one in the file wins. Anything unparseable -> stderr `siteaudit: <leaf>: bad rule line <n>` (1-based), exit 64, no report. Missing rules file -> `siteaudit: rules file not found: <path>`, exit 66.
2. Severity levels with a `-MinSeverity <info|warn|error>` filter (default info). Built-in levels: oversize=error, stale=warn, badname=info. Findings below the floor are dropped. A bad level value -> stderr `siteaudit: bad -MinSeverity value: <v>`, exit 64.

Report format: when NEITHER new flag is used, the classic `<check> <rel>` lines, byte-identical to today. As soon as -Rules or -MinSeverity appears, lines become `<level> <check> <rel>`. Either way all lines sort ordinal, exit 65 iff at least one finding is REPORTED (post-exclusion, post-floor), 0 otherwise.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_siteaudit.ps1` from the workspace root. The test file is not yours to edit.
notes: 6P7 item 5: audit tool grows exclusions + severity with pinned precedence. files/ ships the WORKING classic auditor (14 checks green at baseline: full report, custom thresholds, strict > boundaries via a 4096-byte file and an exactly-90-days file, clean root, 64/66 errors); verify fails only on the -Rules/-MinSeverity surface. Precedence teeth: exclude-beats-severity (archive/* excluded despite no severity), last-severity-wins (assets/Logo* error then assets/Logo Old.png info -> info), patterns with spaces, and a case-sensitivity pin (Archive/* and *.CSV match nothing -- requires -clike or WildcardPattern with case, since -like would fold). Report gains the level column ONLY when a new flag is present, so the classic format stays byte-frozen for the weekly job. Determinism: -Now argument (wave law), mtimes via SetLastWriteTimeUtc, ordinal sort. Differentiation: ps-acctaudit audits account databases (parse-heavy, JSON out); this audits a filesystem tree and its feature is the rules/severity layer grown onto working code; py-retention-lint lints POLICY files, not trees.
